sentenceFrame: The train is in the rain.
celebration: 🎉 LEVEL 3 BEGINS — the Reading Year. The first vowel team: ai and ay, two letters for one long-a sound. The Pattern Tree's RIGHT branches open — leaf #17, and the first leaf of the canopy.

songs.title: Ay-Ay-Play!
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(ay… ay… ay…)
Ay! Ay! Play!

[Hook]
A-I in the middle! (rain! tail!)
A-Y at the end! (day! play!)
Ay-ay-play! Ay-ay-play!
Two letters, one sound! (ay!)

[Verse 2]
R-A-I-N — rain! (in the rain!)
D-A-Y — day! (all day!)
Ay-ay-play! Ay-ay-play!
The A says its name! (ay!)

[Verse 3]
Play in the rain! (splash!)
A train! A tail! (choo-choo!)
Ay-ay-play! Ay-ay-play!
It's a rainy day!

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! In the rain!
(ay… ay… ay…)

[Outro]
Rain… day… play… (shhh…)
Ay! Ay! Play!
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: First Level-3 vowel-team song. ai and ay BOTH sing the long-a /eɪ/ (phonetic respelling: sung 'ay', printed ai/ay) — never letter names. The positional rule is the whole lesson: 'A-I in the middle, A-Y at the end.' Spell-outs uppercase (R-A-I-N). Potato-in-the-rain gag. Cumulative frame reuse: the 'It's a ___!' answer carries from Level 1.

songs.title: The Train in the Rain
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(We can read. We can read!)

[Hook]
The train is in the rain!
The train is in the rain!
Rain, rain! (splash! splash!)
The train is in the rain!

[Verse 2]
Sheep can play in the rain!
Snake has a long tail!
Rain, rain! (splash! splash!)
Play in the rain all day!

[Verse 3]
The train can go! (choo-choo!)
Chick is on the train! (all aboard!)
Rain, rain! (splash! splash!)
Play in the rain all day!

[Outro]
The train… the rain… (shhh…)
In the rain?! (a potato!)
(POTATO!)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: Frame: 'The train is in the rain.' — every content word carries the new ai/ay team or is Level-1 known. Sheep, Snake and Chick play in the storm. Whisper the outro; potato hits loud with a tiny umbrella.

spreads.artNote: A cheerful little steam train alone in the spotlight, headlamp glowing. The first stage of Level 3.
Fat silver raindrops falling through the spotlight, streaks and splashes. Weather arrives.

spreads.text: The train is in the rain.
spreads.artNote: The little train chugging through the downpour, steam and rain mixing, headlamp bright.

spreads.text: Sheep can play in the rain.
spreads.artNote: Sheep — fluffy round white sheep with kind dark eyes — jumping in a puddle, wool soaked, overjoyed.
The bright red caboose at the train's tail end, a lantern swinging from it.
Snake — emerald-green, amber-eyed — proudly showing off its very long tail curled across the platform, matching the train's.
Sheep, Snake and Chick splashing together in the rain beside the train, pure delight.

A wide happy scene: the train, the puddles, the cast dancing in the rain under a grey-gold sky. (Text note: 'all' is untaught /ɔːl/-family — kept out of print; the song may sing 'all day' but the book never prints it.)
A single potato stands in a puddle under a tiny umbrella, perfectly dry and pleased. No noun printed — the class SHOUTS it. Sheep peers around the caboose.

sentences: The train is in the rain.
Sheep can play in the rain.
Play in the rain all day!
extras: THE FIRST VOWEL TEAM. Two vowels walk together and make one long sound — 'when two vowels go walking, the first one does the talking' (a says its name /eɪ/). Show ai and ay side by side.
POSITIONAL RULE: ai lives in the MIDDLE of a word (rain, tail, train); ay lives at the END (day, play, way). Sort a pile of picture cards into two baskets — middle vs end.
THE PATTERN TREE'S RIGHT BRANCH OPENS: hang the ai/ay leaf as leaf #17 — the first of the Level-3 canopy. The left branch (Level 2) is full; now the tree grows the other way.
EAL note: ai/ay is the tense long-a — a clean contrast with the short a of 'cat' from Level 1. This tense-vowel sound feeds the Week-50 tense-lax review; name the pair (cat/came, ran/rain) now.
Moveable alphabet: build r-ai-n, t-r-ai-n, d-ay, p-l-ay — weld the vowel team as one piece.

teacherFocus: LEVEL 3 OPENS — the Reading Year, and the first vowel team. Two vowels together make one long sound: ai and ay both say long-a /eɪ/. Teach the positional rule hard, because it is reliable: ai in the MIDDLE (rain, tail, train), ay at the END (day, play, way). Weld the team as one piece in the moveable alphabet. Open the Pattern Tree's right branch with leaf #17. EAL: this is a tense long-a — pair it out loud with the short a of Level 1 (ran/rain, cat/came); it is the first deposit toward the Week-50 tense-lax review.
